video:video.video:title: Sirens sound to warn of an incoming missile in central Israel
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded to warn of an incoming missile in Central Israel on Wednesday. An AP reporter heard loud booms in Tel Aviv as the windows of buildings shook from reverberations.

video:video.video:title: More strikes hit Tehran as plumes of smoke rise above Iran's capital
video:video.video:description: Airstrikes continued to hit Tehran followed by plumes of smoke rising above buildings in Iran's capital.

video:video.video:title: Rocket lifts off with four Artemis II astronauts on a mission to the moon and back
video:video.video:description: Four Artemis II mission astronauts launched into space from Florida's Kennedy Space Center for humanity’s first trip to the moon in more than half a century.

video:video.video:title: Pioneer Programs
video:video.video:description: Pioneer Programs

video:video.video:title: Danish warship sunk by Admiral Nelson's fleet unearthed after 200 years
video:video.video:description: Marine archaeologists are racing to unearth the Danish warship Dannebroge, which took heavy fire from British Admiral Horatio Nelson’s fleet and later exploded during the 1801 Battle of Copenhagen.

video:video.video:title: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors
video:video.video:description: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors

video:video.video:title: Windsurfer crashes into whale
video:video.video:description: An onlooker captured footage of a windsurfer colliding with a whale in the San Francisco Bay

video:video.video:title: Retirees find gig work to help pay for bills
video:video.video:description: After concluding decades-long careers at hospitals, universities and corporations, retirees are returning to the workforce due to insufficient retirement savings, rising living costs and a desire to stay active.

video:video.video:title: University buildings in Tehran destroyed in US-Israeli strike as Trump ultimatum nears
video:video.video:description: Israel and the United States carried out a wave of attacks on Iran on Monday, killing more than 25 people.

video:video.video:title: Bangladesh implements energy austerity measures amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: Bangladesh imposed energy austerity measures Friday that included cutting office hours and mall opening hours.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes the mood as Trump attends Supreme Court birthright citizenship hearing
video:video.video:description: It was an unprecedented moment as the president attended the Supreme Court arguments. The justices hearing the case included some he appointed.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Rio favela witnesses Good Friday tradition as the Christian world celebrates Holy Week
video:video.video:description: Dozens of residents in one of Rio de Janeiro’s largest favela gathered to follow a Good Friday procession through the narrow streets of Complexo do Alemão.

video:video.video:title: Eggs for Easter cost much less in the US than last year
video:video.video:description: U.S. egg prices have fallen 60% from last year’s record highs, making it easier for consumers to fill their Easter baskets and Passover Seder plates.

video:video.video:title: Jon Batiste discusses London residency
video:video.video:description: Jon Batiste says he’s entering a “creative flow state” during his London residency. He plays at the KOKO venue June 24 - 28.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu offers condolences for four people killed in Iranian attack on Haifa
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday expressed his condolences over victims killed by an Iranian missile attack in Haifa.

video:video.video:title: Dani Bowman talks life beyond ‘Love on the Spectrum’
video:video.video:description: "Can you believe it?" Here's what Dani Bowman says happened after appearing on the Netflix reality series "Love on the Spectrum."

video:video.video:title: A zoo in Chile stages an Easter egg hunt with treats for the animals
video:video.video:description: Chile’s largest private zoo marked Easter Sunday by having several of its animals search for their food during an annual event that resembles an easter egg hunt and is meant to entertain visitors and stimulate the natural behaviors of various species.

video:video.video:title: Tiger Woods says he'll seek treatment after pleading not guilty to DUI
video:video.video:description: Woods pleaded not guilty in his driving under the influence case in Florida on Tuesday, hours after a sheriff’s report said deputies found two pain pills in his pocket and he showed signs of impairment after his SUV clipped a trailer and rolled over on its side four days ago.

video:video.video:title: Trump offered few new details on military operation against Iran in address to nation
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said U.S. forces will keep hitting Iran “very hard” in the next two or three weeks and bring the country “back to the Stone Ages,” even as he touted the success of U.S. operations and argued that all of Washington’s objectives have so far been met or exceeded.

video:video.video:title: Artemis moon mission astronauts wave goodbye and head to the launch pad
video:video.video:description: Artemis astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen leave the Neil Armstrong Operations and Checkout Building and make their way to launch pad 39B

video:video.video:title: Judge tosses Blake Lively’s sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni
video:video.video:description: A federal judge has tossed out Blake Lively’s sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni. But the judge allowed three claims, including retaliation, to move forward.

video:video.video:title: Storms and heavy rainfall disrupt transportation in Afghanistan.
video:video.video:description: Storms and heavy rainfall began across Afghanistan about 12 days ago, affecting most of the country’s 34 provinces.

video:video.video:title: Egg prices drop ahead of Easter after last year’s surge
video:video.video:description: Egg prices in the U.S. have dropped about 60% from last year’s record highs — just in time for Easter. Here’s what’s behind the decline.

video:video.video:title: San Francisco leaders rally against Supreme Court conversion therapy ruling
video:video.video:description: LGBTQ leaders and elected officials rallied on the steps of San Francisco City Hall, responding forcefully to a U.S.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Space fans celebrate as Artemis II rocket launches into space
video:video.video:description: Onlookers across the U.S. watch and celebrate as humanity’s first lunar voyage in more than half a century takes off in NASA’s push toward a landing in two years.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes why Supreme Court seems poised to reject Trump's birthright citizenship limits
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court seems poised to reject President Donald Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship in a consequential case that was magnified by his unparalleled presence in the courtroom.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Iran’s state TV airs video showing what it claims are parts of American aircraft that they shot down
video:video.video:description: As the United States on Sunday said it rescued a service member missing behind enemy lines since Iran downed a fighter jet, Iran’s state TV aired a video showing what it claimed were parts of American aircraft that were part of the rescue operation that the country's military had shot down.

video:video.video:title: Another bite of '28 Years Later' for Taylor-Johnson?
video:video.video:description: Will we see "28 Years Later" actor Aaron Taylor-Johnson team up with the franchise's original star, Cillian Murphy, in future horror sequels?

video:video.video:title: Marilyn Monroe’s final signed check offers insight into the day she died
video:video.video:description: Marilyn Monroe's final signed check is among a number of rare collectibles featured in an upcoming exhibit dedicated to the legendary star.

video:video.video:title: Psychiatric hospital in Tehran damaged by shrapnel from nearby strike
video:video.video:description: A psychiatric hospital in Tehran was damaged by recent Israeli-U.S. strikes in the area where it's located, Iranian state media reported.

video:video.video:title: Unique chocolate creations to mark Easter and promote Belgian chocolate are for sell in Brussels
video:video.video:description: To celebrate Easter and promote Belgian chocolate, nearly 40 chocolatiers from all over Belgium put their best effort to create unusual and unique pieces.

video:video.video:title: Machete-wielding man attacks Ugandan nursery school, killing 4 children
video:video.video:description: A man killed four children in a machete attack inside a nursery school in the Ugandan capital of Kampala on Thursday, police said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 6
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, April 6th: Trump praises rescue of U.S. airmen in Iran; Trump and first lady take part in White House egg roll; former governor of Russia's Kursk region sentenced to prison; astronauts set record as the father humans have ever traveled from earth.

video:video.video:title: Georgia secretary of state says investors who lost money in financial scheme will get $6.7 million
video:video.video:description: Eight months after the collapse of an alleged $140 million Ponzi scheme, Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger announced a third of the victims will obtain a $6.7 million settlement.

video:video.video:title: Cuba introduces new banknotes honoring women to ease inflation as crises persist
video:video.video:description: Residents accustomed to shopping with wads of bills stuffed into bags because of skyrocketing inflation received some relief as two new high-denomination banknotes went into circulation.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 3
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 3rd: NASA astronauts fire engines to head toward the moon; Record fuel price increases in Pakistan; Heavy rains down bridges in Italy; Marshmallows dropped from a helicopter in Michigan

video:video.video:title: Israeli authorities respond in three cities after latest Iranian missile attacks
video:video.video:description: Emergency workers were at the scene Monday of several damage sites in Israel after officials said multiple cities were hit by Iranian missiles.

video:video.video:title: Fired librarian says moving LGBTQ-themed children's books is a First Amendment violation
video:video.video:description: A fired Tennessee librarian stands by her decision not to move LGBTQ-themed books from the children's section, which she said would have violated the First Amendment.

video:video.video:title: Trump heads to Supreme Court, making him the first sitting president to attend top court's arguments
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has left the White House, heading to the Supreme Court. Trump will be the first sitting president to attend oral arguments at the nation’s highest court.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Kanye West fans gather for LA stadium show despite controversies
video:video.video:description: Here's why fans say they showed up to Ye's SoFi Stadium concert to hear the artist formerly known as Kanye West following years of controversies.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy concerned prolonged US-Israeli war on Iran could further erode US support for Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy expressed concern that a prolonged U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could further erode America’s support for Ukraine as Washington’s global priorities shift and Kyiv braces for reduced deliveries of critically needed Patriot air defence missiles.

video:video.video:title: Trump claims Iran wants a ceasefire, Iran says remarks are ‘false and baseless’
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he would bomb Iran "back to the Stone Ages" if the Islamic Republic doesn't open the Strait of Hormuz -- his latest verbal U-turn in the war.

video:video.video:title: Scientists figured out why sneakers squeak on basketball courts
video:video.video:description: A new study uses physics to uncover why sneakers squeak on the basketball court. The findings can help scientists understand essential questions about friction, which has important practical applications.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III and UK royal family members attend Easter service at Windsor Castle
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla attended an Easter service in Windsor on Sunday. They were joined by the Prince and Princess of Wales and their children.

video:video.video:title: Tensions remain high at Israeli-Lebanese border as Israel outlines plans for invasion
video:video.video:description: Stefanie Black lives in the northern Israeli Kibbutz of Amir and said that "people feel unsafe here".

video:video.video:title: Five dead in fire at a gas lighter factory near Dhaka
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out at a factory manufacturing gas lighters near Bangladesh’s capital on Saturday, leaving at least five people dead, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: ACLU lawyers confident Supreme Court will reject Trump’s birthright citizenship limits
video:video.video:description: Lawyers with the American Civil Liberties Union say they believe the Supreme Court will reject President Donald Trump's restrictions on birthright citizenship.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Danish warship sunk by Admiral Nelson's fleet unearthed after 200 years
video:video.video:description: Marine archaeologists are racing to unearth the Danish warship Dannebroge, which took heavy fire from British Admiral Horatio Nelson’s fleet and later exploded during the 1801 Battle of Copenhagen.

video:video.video:title: Iran official calls for youths to gather at power plants ahead of threatened Trump strikes
video:video.video:description: An Iranian official early Tuesday issued a video message calling on youths of the Islamic Republic to form "human chains" around power plants in the country ahead of threatened strikes by U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump marvels at drop in price of eggs at White House Easter Egg roll
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump welcomed thousands to the annual White House Easter Egg roll Monday.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV remembers Pope Francis in Easter Monday blessing
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV remembered his predecessor Pope Francis’s "profound witness of faith and love" during the traditional Easter Monday blessing.

video:video.video:title: Sexual violence being used as weapon in Sudan's war, doctors group says
video:video.video:description: Sudan slid into chaos in April 2023 when a power struggle between the two forces exploded into open fighting in Khartoum and elsewhere.

video:video.video:title: At least 10 arrested as hundreds gather for anti-war protest in Tel Aviv
video:video.video:description: Police forcibly dispersed anti-war protesters in Tel Aviv on Saturday amid wartime restrictions and arrested at least 10 people.

video:video.video:title: Aid groups warn Iran war is hindering food and medicine from reaching millions
video:video.video:description: Aid groups warned the war in the Middle East has upended their ability to get food and medicine to millions of people around the world in need, and that the suffering will deepen if the violence continues.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds protest U.S. blockade in Cuba as Russia announces more aid
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of bicycles, motorcycles, and small three-wheeled electric vehicles that have become Havana’s main means of transportation paraded Thursday along the Havana Bay seawall to protest the U.S. embargo.

video:video.video:title: Women reclaim the weight room from toxic masculinity
video:video.video:description: Women are taking over some of the heavy lifting from the “gym bros” with women-only gyms designed to combat toxic masculinity.

video:video.video:title: Local officials in Iran said the B1 bridge struck on Thursday was not used for military purposes
video:video.video:description: Local officials in charge of construction of the B1 bridge that was attacked on Thursday in Iran, said it was not used for military purposes.

video:video.video:title: Trump says it would take 4 hours to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants
video:video.video:description: The president described the consequences that Iran would face if it didn’t reach a deal with the U.S. by Trump’s 8 p.m. Tuesday deadline.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 1
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 1st: NASA readies Artemis II moon rocket for launch; Iran's foreign minister acknowledges messages from U.S. envoy; Germany set limits on gas price hikes at the pump; Trump at Supreme Court oral arguments on birthright citizenship
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Starmer says UK to host Strait of Hormuz summit, and country committed to NATO after Trump criticism
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the U.K. will host an international diplomatic conference this week on ways to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Asked about U.S.

video:video.video:title: Latin Patriarch leads Holy Thursday prayers at the Holy Sepulcher church without public attendance
video:video.video:description: Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa of Jerusalem washed and dried the feet of clergy, as Israel’s police said it had approved a “limited prayer framework” for the church.

video:video.video:title: Lost dog rescued by helicopter in New Zealand after strangers fund search
video:video.video:description: When a hiker fell from a 55-meter (180-foot) waterfall in wild New Zealand bush, rescuers were forced to evacuate the badly hurt woman without her dog, which couldn't be found.

video:video.video:title: Fossil discovery rewrites history of complex life
video:video.video:description: A massive fossil discovery in southwest China has shifted our understanding of when complex animals first appeared on Earth.

video:video.video:title: Trump brushes off war crimes concerns as he widens threat to Iran's power plants and bridges
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday he’s “not at all” concerned about committing possible war crimes as he threatened to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants if Tehran does not meet his Tuesday 8 p.m.

video:video.video:title: DNA testing links teen's 1974 murder to serial killer Ted Bundy
video:video.video:description: Utah authorities say new DNA testing has definitively linked the unsolved death of a Utah teenager in 1974 to the infamous serial killer Ted Bundy.

video:video.video:title: Universities pressured to strip names of Epstein associates from campus buildings
video:video.video:description: Protests are growing at universities where buildings bear the names of associates of the late sexual predator Jeffrey Epstein.

video:video.video:title: US lifts sanctions on Delcy Rodríguez, Venezuela’s acting president
video:video.video:description: The U.S. on Wednesday lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting President Delcy Rodríguez, according to an Office of Foreign Assets Control entry on the Treasury Department website.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 3
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 3rd: One crew member rescued after US fighter jet is shot down in Iran; Israeli airstrikes kill nearly two-dozen in Lebanon; US employers added 178,000 jobs last month; Artemis II's astronauts capture Earth's blue beauty in photos

video:video.video:title: Marilyn Monroe collection reveals personal life
video:video.video:description: Items from the Marilyn Monroe Icon Collection were on display at a press preview at the Waldorf Astoria New York recently, including personal documents that reveal her private life and one of the last items she signed.

video:video.video:title: Israel Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field
video:video.video:description: Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field

video:video.video:title: Israeli authorities recover bodies from building hit by Iranian missile
video:video.video:description: Israeli rescuers on Monday removed bodies of people from the rubble of a building hit by an Iranian missile in Haifa on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors
video:video.video:description: Jonathan, believed to be 193, is still kicking — albeit slowly — on the island of St. Helena. “It was a hoax,” Anne Dillon, head of communications on the island, told The Associated Press on Thursday about his alleged passing.
video:video.video:tag: St. Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha

video:video.video:title: Suicide bomber attack kills at least five at police station in northwestern Pakistan
video:video.video:description: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a police station in the Bannu district of northwestern Pakistan late Thursday, killing at least five people and wounding several others, local police chief Rafi Ullah said.

video:video.video:title: FIFA World Cup tickets near $11,000 as prices surge
video:video.video:description: FIFA World Cup final ticket prices are climbing to nearly $11,000 — far higher than previous tournaments. Here’s what’s behind the surge.

video:video.video:title: Bridge in Iran struck by US not used by military, local officials say
video:video.video:description: Iran state media reported that a US strike on the B1 bridge killed at least eight people and wounded 95.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II engine firing was 'flawless,' NASA says
video:video.video:description: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts fired their engines and blazed toward the moon Thursday night, breaking free of Earth orbit for the first time since 1972.

video:video.video:title: Video shows Tiger Woods being arrested after Florida crash
video:video.video:description: Newly released video shows Tiger Woods hiccuping and appearing to nod off in the back of a patrol car after crashing his vehicle last week in Florida.

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents picnic in parks on last day of Nowruz despite airstrikes on country
video:video.video:description: The annual public picnic day, called Sizdeh Bedar, which comes from the Farsi words for “thirteen” and “day out,” is a legacy from Iran’s pre-Islamic past that hard-liners in the Islamic Republic never managed to erase from calendars.

video:video.video:title: Eyes are on the Bab el-Mandeb Strait after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday made expletive-filled threats against Iran and its infrastructure if it doesn't open the Strait of Hormuz by his Tuesday deadline, after American forces rescued a wounded aviator whose Iran-downed plane fell behind enemy lines.

video:video.video:title: China company offers glimpse into how AI could help reduce impact of synthetic textile waste
video:video.video:description: In an industrial park in Zhangjiagang, a small city on China's east coast, a large humming and hissing machine feeds on piles of used clothes and sorts them.

video:video.video:title: As China develops renewable energy, its coal miners are facing the pressure of major change
video:video.video:description: As China prioritizes renewable energy over coal, miners are being forced to confront uncertainty. (AP video shot by: Wayne Zhang)

video:video.video:title: Animals killed after Russia strike damages Kyiv region veterinary clinic
video:video.video:description: Animals were killed after a Russian strike damaged a veterinary clinic in the Kyiv region on Friday, the State Emergency Service reported.

video:video.video:title: BeWell: Some young men are discovering the wellbeing benefits of crocheting
video:video.video:description: In Italy some young men are embracing crafts traditionally associated with women, such as crochet, discovering the wellbeing benefits of mindful crafting.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II crew share emotional moment over loved one lost after breaking record
video:video.video:description: Moments after breaking Apollo 13’s record, the astronauts asked permission to name two fresh lunar craters already observed.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13’s distance record with daring moon flyby that included a solar eclipse
video:video.video:description: After traveling deeper into space than any other humans, the Artemis II astronauts pointed their moon ship toward home Monday night, wrapping up a lunar cruise that revealed views of the far side never beheld by eyes until now.

video:video.video:title: Chester Zoo welcomes the birth of one of world's tiniest antelopes
video:video.video:description: Zookeepers at Chester Zoo said Dotty will reach just 40cm in height when fully grown. The fawn will be raised by a surrogate father after her biological father died of old age shortly before her birth.

video:video.video:title: Iraq's oil hub slows to crawl as Strait of Hormuz shutdown strangles exports
video:video.video:description: Iraqi oil fields once alive with the buzz of workers are nearly deserted. Ports that pulsed with the churn of cargo have fallen still, the din of commerce replaced by the soft rhythm of waves.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers lose hope for humpback whale stranded in Baltic Sea
video:video.video:description: Rescuers said Wednesday that they have lost hope for the humpback whale that has become stranded repeatedly off Germany's Baltic Sea coast and now expect it to die in the inlet where it currently lies.

video:video.video:title: Fresh starters attend underwater welcoming ceremony in western Japan
video:video.video:description: First of April marks the start of a new fiscal and academic year in Japan, a time widely regarded as a fresh start.

video:video.video:title: Iranian minister dismisses Trump threat in AP interview
video:video.video:description: Iran’s culture minister on Sunday dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump’s latest threats, calling him an “unstable, delusional figure. (AP video shot by Bassam Hatoum)

video:video.video:title: Column of smoke rises skywards in Tehran following latest Israel-US strike
video:video.video:description: Explosions were heard and a column of smoke was seen rising in Tehran early on Wednesday following the latest Israel-U.S. strike on the Iranian capital.

video:video.video:title: What you need to know about the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The narrow, bending waterway in the Persian Gulf is a key trade route between the Middle East and the rest of the world, with global impact.

video:video.video:title: Stay tuned Cincinnati: WKRP is coming to town for real, North Carolina station's director says
video:video.video:description: In a case of life imitating art, there could soon be a real "WKRP in Cincinnati." A North Carolina nonprofit that has used the famous call letters for a low-power radio broadcast has signed a deal to let a group launch a station in the Ohio city that was home to the fictional station from the popula

video:video.video:title: ICT building at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology reduced to rubble after more US-Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: An airstrike hit Monday an information and communication technology building at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology, a professor there said.

video:video.video:title: Dizzying fuel prices are determined by factors largely outside of a gas station's control
video:video.video:description: The near-daily changes in U.S. gas prices have been dizzying for drivers. Experts say differences in price aren’t typically decided by any individual gas retailer, and most of them aren't pocketing the extra pennies when prices rise.

video:video.video:title: Good Friday a solemn day for Christians, with ancient prayers and fervent processions
video:video.video:description: Celebrated the Friday before Easter, Good Friday commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus. This year, it will fall on April 3 for Catholics and Protestants, and April 10 for Orthodox Christians.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts race to set a new distance record from Earth
video:video.video:description: With the moon looming ever larger, the Artemis II astronauts raced to set a new distance record Monday from Earth on a lunar fly-around promising magnificent views of the far side never seen before by eye.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 27 March - 2 April 2026

video:video.video:title: Opening night for Sink & Jupe's ‘Romeo & Juliet’
video:video.video:description: Sadie Sink and Noah Jupe embrace Shakespearean tragedy - and tea - to star in the West End's latest "Romeo & Juliet."

video:video.video:title: Good Friday procession brings crowds to Quito
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of Ecuadorian Christians joined a Good Friday procession through the capital, Quito, on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Anna Cathcart on Lana Condor's ‘XO, Kitty’ appearance
video:video.video:description: Lana Condor revives her "To All the Boys" character for Season 3 of "XO, Kitty." Here's what Anna Cathcart had to say about it.

video:video.video:title: India has begun its long-delayed population census
video:video.video:description: India has begun the world’s largest national population count, which could reshape welfare programs and political representation across the country.

video:video.video:title: Unique Easter tradition lives on in Hungarian village as women doused in buckets of water
video:video.video:description: The small village of Hollókő in northeastern Hungary still observers an ancient Easter tradition to this day, when local men douse women with buckets of water during the holiday.

video:video.video:title: From shore to plate: How Irish seaweed is making waves on restaurant menus
video:video.video:description: Irish seaweed is in demand. Traditionally used in fertilisers, chefs and researchers on Ireland's west coast are advocating for wider uses: on menus, in gardens and cosmetics.

video:video.video:title: Who is Todd Blanche, the new acting Attorney General?
video:video.video:description: Blanche, whom Trump elevated Thursday from deputy attorney general to acting U.S. attorney general, rose to prominence representing the president in criminal cases that consumed the four years between his first and second terms.

video:video.video:title: Trump says the US 'core strategic objectives' in Iran are 'nearing completion'
video:video.video:description: In his first national address since launching the Iran war, President Donald Trump said U.S. forces are "on track to complete" their mission soon, as the “core strategic objectives are nearing completion.

video:video.video:title: Thousands mourn Revolutionary Guard navy commander in Tehran
video:video.video:description: Thousands of mourners gathered in the Iranian capital on Wednesday to bid farewell to Commodore Alireza Tangsiri, the head of the Revolutionary Guard’s navy, a day after his official funeral service.

video:video.video:title: Stocks recover from early losses and close with a weekly gain. US oil tops $110 a barrel
video:video.video:description: Oil prices remained elevated, however, having soared after a national address late Wednesday from President Donald Trump, where he vowed the U.S. will continue to attack Iran.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 1
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 1st: Supreme Court hears arguments in birthright citizenship case; Trump travels to Supreme Court to hear arguments; FDA approves new obesity drug; Artemis II moon rocket readies for historic mission.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Interviews with Artemis II astronauts as they rocket toward the moon
video:video.video:description: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts fired their engines and blazed toward the moon Thursday night, breaking free of the chains that have trapped humanity in shallow laps around Earth in the decades since Apollo.

video:video.video:title: Trump threatens to strike Iran's infrastructure if Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday he would strike Iran’s power plants and bridges this Tuesday if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed to marine traffic.

video:video.video:title: Celebrations in Baghdad as Iraq's soccer team qualifies for 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people celebrated in Bagdad on Saturday as the Iraqi national soccer team returned to the capital following their qualification for the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers lose hope for humpback whale stranded in Baltic Sea
video:video.video:description: Rescuers said Wednesday that they have given up hope for the humpback whale that has become stranded repeatedly off Germany’s Baltic Sea coast and now expect it to die in the inlet where it currently lies.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts reach orbit on historic mission to the moon and back
video:video.video:description: Four astronauts embarked on a high-stakes flight around the moon Wednesday, humanity’s first lunar voyage in more than half a century and the thrilling leadoff in NASA’s push toward a landing in two years.

video:video.video:title: Trump tells countries that rely on oil from Strait of Hormuz 'they must grab it and cherish it'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is seeking to allay fears in the U.S. about rising gas prices as the war on Iran has continues.

video:video.video:title: Video brings new scrutiny to ICE shooting in Minneapolis after charges against 2 men are dropped
video:video.video:description: Federal authorities initially accused two men of beating an ICE officer with a broom handle and a snow shovel before opening an investigation into the officers giving untruthful testimony under oath.

video:video.video:title: Pope in Good Friday Way of the Cross ceremony at Rome's Colosseum
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV personally carried the wooden cross through all 14 stations of the Way of the Cross at the Colosseum on his first Good Friday as pontiff, marking the first time in decades that a pope carries the cross to every station.

video:video.video:title: After a harsh winter, Ukrainians find joy in releasing bats rescued from war
video:video.video:description: As night fell over a nature park on the edge of Kyiv in Ukraine, children crowded around volunteers who carefully opened cloth bags and released bats into the twilight.

video:video.video:title: Israelis mark central ritual of Passover in parking garages as war forces families into shelters
video:video.video:description: Israelis marked the central ritual of Passover in parking garages on Wednesday as the war forced families into shelters.

video:video.video:title: Road to Tehran offers glimpses of destruction and daily life in wartime Iran
video:video.video:description: Associated Press reporters crossed into Iran from Turkey on Saturday after being granted permission by the Iranian government to send an additional team into the country.

video:video.video:title: Surprise! Zendaya wears something blue -- after the old, new and borrowed
video:video.video:description: First she wore something old. Then new, then borrowed — and now blue. Zendaya showed up at her New York premiere for “The Drama” in a dazzling blue Schiaparelli couture ball gown, completing a bridal tease she'd begun weeks ago.

video:video.video:title: University research center in Tehran severely damaged in airstrike
video:video.video:description: A laser and plasma research center in Tehran was severely damaged following an airstrike Friday. Iran's Minister of Science, Research and Technology, Hossein Simaei Sarraf, accused those who carried out the strike of "Stone Age" behavior.

video:video.video:title: Like her 'Something Bad' character, Camila Morrone listens to her gut
video:video.video:description: Camila Morrone trusts her instincts when making big decisions. She stars in the Netflix horror series "Something Very Bad is Going to Happen."

video:video.video:title: Oil prices rocket higher and stocks tumble after Trump’s address
video:video.video:description: Stocks slumped and oil prices soared Thursday after President Donald Trump vowed the U.S. will continue to attack Iran and failed to offer a clear timetable for ending the conflict in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Local affiliate of Iran state TV claims a US fighter pilot ejected from an aircraft in southwest Iran
video:video.video:description: The anchor on the channel read the following announcement: “If you capture the enemy pilot or pilots alive and hand them over to the police, you will receive a precious prize.”

video:video.video:title: Doctor warns that Israel is ravaging Lebanon's health care system
video:video.video:description: Two years ago, Dr. Mohammed Ziara watched Israel ravage Gaza's health care system, shelling hospitals, striking ambulances and forcing patients to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Building damaged in town of Ramat Gan after Iran fires cluster munition towards Israel
video:video.video:description: Rescue teams worked to clear the debris from a damaged home in the central town of Ramat Gan on Saturday after Iran fired what the Israeli army said was a cluster munition towards Israel.

video:video.video:title: A fast-growing wildfire in windy Southern California triggers evacuations
video:video.video:description: A smoky and fast-growing wildfire in Southern California has prompted multiple evacuation orders and warnings.

video:video.video:title: People in Moscow prepare for warm spring expected to break historical temperature records
video:video.video:description: The arrival of spring has brought unusually high temperatures to Russia's capital, to the delight of its residents.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV carries cross for full Good Friday procession, the first pontiff to do so in decades
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV carried a wooden cross for all of the 14 stations of the Way of the Cross at the Colosseum on his first Good Friday as pontiff, marking the first time in decades that a pope carried the cross to every station.

video:video.video:title: Researchers in Mexico develop easy-to-use robotic arm
video:video.video:description: Researchers in Mexico have developed a robotic arm they say reduces the complexity that often leads patients to abandon prosthetic devices.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13's record
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II astronauts are more than halfway to the moon. They are the first moonbound crew in more than half a century, picking up where NASA's Apollo program left off.

video:video.video:title: Effigies burnt as part of the "Burning of Judas" ritual as Mexico marks Easter Saturday
video:video.video:description: Effigies of Donald Trump and other political figures were set alight Saturday evening as part of a "Burning of Judas" celebration in Mexico City.

video:video.video:title: Two Pennsylvania firefighters in UTV killed in head-on crash during search for missing woman
video:video.video:description: Two firefighters in a utility vehicle have died in a head-on crash in Pennsylvania. The accident happened Saturday around 6 p.m. when their utility vehicle collided with a Toyota Camry on Route 222.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth asks the Army's top uniformed officer to step down while US wages war against Iran
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has asked the Army’s top uniformed officer, Gen. Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Drone attack hits fuel warehouse in northern Iraq linked to BP oil and natural gas giant
video:video.video:description: Flames and black smoke billowed from the motor oil storage site in the Kurdish region of Iraq following Wednesday's attack.

video:video.video:title: Christian faithful gather in Gaza City to mark Good Friday
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Christian faithful gathered in the Holy Family Church to mark Good Friday in Gaza City, celebrating in silence and reverence.

video:video.video:title: Pam Bondi is out at US Attorney General
video:video.video:description: Pam Bondi is out as US Attorney General, ending the tenure of a loyalist who upended the Justice Department’s culture of independence from the White House.

video:video.video:title: Markets in Asia shows "disappointment" in Trump's speech on Iran military operation, analyst says
video:video.video:description: Tokyo’s Nikkei 225 was down 1.4% to 53,004.81 in early Asia trading, while South Korea’s Kospi lost 3.4% to 5,292.36 and Hong Kong’s Hang Seng fell 0.8% to 25,082.59.

video:video.video:title: Apollo astronauts brought these items to the moon's surface
video:video.video:description: While Artemis II astronauts make their way around the moon and back, take a look at some Apollo items that have touched the lunar surface.

video:video.video:title: 3 state troopers plead not guilty to charges connected to death of recruit after boxing match
video:video.video:description: A supervisor and two instructors with a Massachusetts State Police tactical unit plead not guilty in connection with the death of a recruit who suffered a concussion during a sparring session and blunt force injuries a day later in what investigators called an “unapproved and unsafe” boxing match.

video:video.video:title: Trump plans to attend Supreme Court hearing on his bid to limit birthright citizenship
video:video.video:description: The citizenship restrictions are a part of Trump’s broader immigration crackdown, signed as an executive order in January 2025, but they have not yet taken effect anywhere in the country after being blocked by several courts.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: 'Wow!' The eye surgery marathon that restored sight for some South Africans
video:video.video:description: An 84-year-old South African woman is among more than 130 patients who had their sight restored during a free cataract surgery “marathon” aimed at tackling long backlogs in the public health system.

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage shows Tiger Woods being arrested at scene of car crash
video:video.video:description: Bodycam video shows Tiger Woods being placed in hand cuffs on suspicion of driving under the influence after his vehicle crashed in Florida on March 27th.

video:video.video:title: Designer Easter treats in stunning colours and shapes
video:video.video:description: A chocolatier in Barcelona has created high-end traditional Catalan treats for Easter. Known as 'monas', they can come in a variety of shapes and flavours.

video:video.video:title: Why Jonah Hill chose Keanu Reeves to be canceled in 'The Outcome'
video:video.video:description: Why Jonah Hill chose Keanu Reeves to be canceled in 'The Outcome'

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage shows Tiger Woods being arrested
video:video.video:description: Bodycam video shows Tiger Woods being placed in hand cuffs on suspicion of driving under the influence after his vehicle crashed in Florida on March 27th.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, April 5: Trump threatens Iran over the Strait of Hormuz.; Israeli‑Lebanese border tensions high; Artemis II aims to go farther from Earth than Apollo 13; Pope Leo XIV calls for peace during his first Easter Mass.

video:video.video:title: CCTV footage appears to show kidnapping of US journalist Shelly Kittleson in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Shelly Kittleson, a freelance journalist who has worked for years in Iraq and Syria, remains missing after her abduction on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Woman escapes from police car while handcuffed
video:video.video:description: A woman who shimmied out the rear window of a police cruiser while handcuffed and then fled over the weekend was arrested Tuesday, authorities in western Michigan said.

video:video.video:title: Beirut neighborhood hit overnight in fatal Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed in an Israeli strike early Wednesday in Beirut's Jnah neighborhood.

video:video.video:title: Tanker truck in Texas erupts into fiery blaze after collision
video:video.video:description: A tanker truck in Texas hauling gasoline erupted into a fiery blaze on Sunday morning after colliding with another vehicle and knocking over power lines, leaving the truck driver in critical condition.

video:video.video:title: Why 'unretired' seniors are picking up gig work to pay the bills
video:video.video:description: A growing number of Americans have “unretired” in recent years. After ending careers that spanned decades, they returned to the workforce due to rising living costs, insufficient retirement savings, and a desire to stay active.

video:video.video:title: JetBlue raises its checked bag fees as the Iran war affects fuel costs
video:video.video:description: U.S. carrier JetBlue has raised its checked bag fees by as much as $9 as the war in the Middle East disrupts global oil supplies and increases fuel prices.

video:video.video:title: Sky turns eerie red as storm blows Sahara sand dust into atmosphere in Greece
video:video.video:description: The skies over the Greek island of Crete took on Martian hues on Wednesday. The eerie spectacle was the result of strong winds blowing stifling dust across the Mediterranean from North Africa.

video:video.video:title: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors
video:video.video:description: Jonathan the tortoise, believed to be 193, is still kicking - albeit slowly - on the island of St. Helena despite rumors of his death.
video:video.video:tag: St. Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha

video:video.video:title: The oldest juke joint in Mississippi keeping the blues alive since 1948
video:video.video:description: The oldest juke joint in Mississippi, and perhaps the whole of the U.S., is still keeping the blues alive 78 years after first opening its doors.

video:video.video:title: Record low Colorado mountain snow threatens grim year for dry US West
video:video.video:description: Hydrologists working high in the Rocky Mountains have measured what they say is Colorado’s driest winter of snow moisture on record.

video:video.video:title: After crash, Tiger Woods told deputy he was 'talking to the president,' body camera shows
video:video.video:description: After crashing his SUV last week in Florida, Tiger Woods took out his phone and told a deputy, “I was just talking to the president,” according to body camera footage released Thursday showing Woods' arrest on a DUI charge.

video:video.video:title: Man indicted in connection with 2 'Texas Killing Fields' cases
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors say a man they believe is connected to some of the “Texas Killing Fields” deaths of dozens of women found near Houston since the 1970s has been indicted.

video:video.video:title: A native seabird population is surging in Honolulu, finding a home among office towers and traffic
video:video.video:description: Manu-o-Kū, a seabird native to Hawaii, is flourishing in the middle of Honolulu's concrete towers, traffic-clogged roads and Waikiki hotels, defying the fate of many other native birds in Hawaii.

video:video.video:title: Streets of Beirut filled with displaced people as Israel continues military operation
video:video.video:description: It's been a month since Hezbollah fired rockets into Israel after the U.S.-Israeli attack on its patron, Iran, triggering Israeli bombardment of Lebanon and a ground invasion.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans celebrates Easter Sunday with a rainy French Quarter Parade
video:video.video:description: Parade-goers traveled to the historic streets of the French Quarter in New Orleans for an annual Easter Sunday parade in the rain. (AP Video: Stephen Smith)

video:video.video:title: Myanmar's ruling general elected as President, keeping army in charge
video:video.video:description: Myanmar’s parliament on Friday elected Min Aung Hlaing, a general who ousted Aung San Suu Kyi’s civilian government in 2021 and kept an iron grip on power for the past five years, as the country’s new president.

video:video.video:title: Remains of three Indonesian United Nations peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon arrive in Jakarta
video:video.video:description: The remains of three Indonesian United Nations peacekeepers who were killed while on escort duty supporting U.N. operations in southern Lebanon arrived in Jakarta on Saturday evening, where President Prabowo Subianto led a solemn welcome ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Mamdani says new rental ripoff hearings are the start of NYC cracking down on 'bad landlords'
video:video.video:description: New York mayor Zohran Mamdani says his new “rental ripoff hearings" are the start of the city's crack down on "bad landlords" but many who attended said the hearing needs to be the beginning, not the end of an age-old New York problem.

video:video.video:title: A masterclass in traditional Ukrainian fresh egg decorating
video:video.video:description: The main symbol of Easter in Ukraine is the pysanka, a fresh egg decorated with patterns and symbols which are unique to each region.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II's astronauts capture Earth's brilliant blue beauty on the way to the moon
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II astronauts captured our blue planet’s brilliant beauty as they zoom ever closer to the moon. (AP produced by Javier Arciga)

video:video.video:title: Judge blocks Trump’s $400M White House ballroom plan
video:video.video:description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to suspend construction of a $400 million ballroom it demolished the East Wing of the White House to make space for, barring work from proceeding without congressional approval.

video:video.video:title: 'We saw some extraordinary things' - astronauts give briefing aboard Artemis II
video:video.video:description: Astronauts on board the Artemis II spacecraft described on Saturday the "extraordinary things" they had already seen a few days into their mission.

video:video.video:title: Cold War life on show in a former Communist-era bomb shelter
video:video.video:description: A museum located in an old bomb shelter is providing visitors with a glimpse of what is was like to live in Communist Albania during the Cold War.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese Christians mark Easter away from their southern border villages
video:video.video:description: In Lebanon, home to the largest proportion of Christians of any country in the Arab world, most Christians living in the war-torn south are celebrating this Easter in the shadow of a war they want nothing to do with, far from the churches their families attended for generations.

video:video.video:title: Looking for a spring detox? Greek monks have a centuries-old playbook
video:video.video:description: Across Greece and in Orthodox communities around the world, a centuries-old Lenten fast offers a modern lesson in diet and discipline.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13’s distance record as humans travel farther from Earth than ever before
video:video.video:description: The previous title was held by the Apollo 13 crew, who logged a maximum distance of 248,655 miles from Earth.

video:video.video:title: How Graham Platner is building momentum with trivia games and happy hours in Maine's Senate race
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner has not only refused to bow out of Maine's U.S. Senate race, but has continued to attract supporters willing to forgive his past transgressions and enthusiastically embrace his populist message.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's 'not at all' concerned about war crimes as he threatens Iran's infrastructure
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday he’s “not at all” concerned about committing possible war crimes as he continued to threaten the destruction of Iran’s bridges and power plants if they don’t meet his Tuesday 8 p.m.

video:video.video:title: Same sex couples marry in Amsterdam as Dutch mark 25th anniversary of world's first gay weddings
video:video.video:description: The Dutch capital marked the 25th anniversary of the world's first gay marriages with three same-sex couples tying the knot at City Hall early Wednesday.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: A little roar of hope: Zoo Knoxville’s hand‑raised lion cub
video:video.video:description: A male African lion cub born at Zoo Knoxville is being hand‑raised after nursing challenges with his first‑time mother.

video:video.video:title: Snowstorms and heavy rain batter Italy
video:video.video:description: Firefighters used special snowcat trucks to plow through huge banks of snow in small towns in the Italian region of Molise, while the Trigno River in Campobasso burst its banks on Thursday causing a bridge collapse.

video:video.video:title: US employers added a surprisingly strong 178,000 jobs last month, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: American employers added a surprisingly strong 178,000 new jobs last month, rebounding from a dismal February. And the unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II breaks distance record as humans travel farther from Earth than ever before
video:video.video:description: Artemis II kicks off trip around the moon after surpassing Apollo 13’s distance record. Artemis II astronauts also got a special message from Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell, who recorded the message just two months before his death last August.

video:video.video:title: Funeral held for man killed in Israeli drone attack on Gaza City
video:video.video:description: An Israeli drone killed a Palestinian man Monday in the Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said. The strike hit a bike in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood in Gaza City and also wounded a child, according to the Shifa hospital which received the casualties.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II kicks off its trip around the moon
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II astronauts have started their record-breaking trip around the moon that already is providing unprecedented views of the far side.

video:video.video:title: Trump praises 'historic' US airman rescue, warns Iran could be 'taken out in one night'
video:video.video:description: Trump praises 'historic' US airman rescue, warns Iran could be 'taken out in one night'

video:video.video:title: Trump insists Iranians want the US to 'keep bombing'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday the Iranian people want the U.S. to "keep bombing," insisting citizens are “willing to suffer" in order to secure their freedom.

video:video.video:title: Nitrate pollution, agriculture’s persistent problem, expected to worsen with warming winters
video:video.video:description: As Earth warms due to human-caused climate change, the ground isn’t staying frozen as consistently in many places, causing more frequent runoff events and in turn, more winter days when nitrates are likely to reach unhealthy levels.

video:video.video:title: Record low snowpack in Colorado could spell trouble for millions in the U.S. West
video:video.video:description: Hydrologists working high in the Rocky Mountains have measured what they say was Colorado’s worst snowpack on record—an alarming fact for a state that supplies drinking water to millions across the drought-stricken American West.

video:video.video:title: Iranian TV claims a US pilot ejected over Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. did not respond immediately to requests for comment on the claim. It was not clear what may have happened to the plane, including whether Iran was claiming it was shot down or had another issue.

video:video.video:title: AP journalists witness widespread destruction at Shiite religious complex in Zanjan
video:video.video:description: Journalists from The Associated Press on Saturday witnessed widespread destruction at a Shiite religious complex in Zanjan, a city in northwestern Iran.

video:video.video:title: Panicked locals flee homes and offices as earthquake rattles northern Indonesia
video:video.video:description: An earthquake in Indonesian waters set off small tsunami waves on Thursday morning, killing at least one person and damaging houses and buildings, officials said.

video:video.video:title: English choirs seek UNESCO recognition for evensong, a musical tradition
video:video.video:description: A campaign is underway to protect England's choral music tradition, which has thrived for nearly 500 years.

video:video.video:title: Children dash to gather marshmallows dropped from a helicopter at two Detroit-area parks
video:video.video:description: It rained marshmallows at a pair of Detroit-area parks as kids raced to collect thousands of the fluffy treats dropped from the sky. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Thousands walk in largest Good Friday pilgrimage in the US
video:video.video:description: During Holy Week and particularly on Good Friday, tens of thousands of people make the pilgrimage to the northern New Mexico village of Chimayó.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Lebanese Christian village in shock following deadly Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: The hilly Christian town of Ain Saadeh east of Beirut was in shock Monday after an Israeli airstrike crashed into an apartment building there, killing an anti-Hezbollah politician, his wife and another woman.

video:video.video:title: Infiniti hopes 'The Testaments' will inspire
video:video.video:description: Actor Chase Infiniti shares her hopes that her new series "The Testaments" - the highly anticipated spin off from "The Handmaid's Tale" - will have a lasting impact on young women.

video:video.video:title: Israel’s rescue services search for people after Iran strikes Haifa apartment building
video:video.video:description: Israel’s Fire and Rescue Authority said Sunday they are searching in northern Israel for three people who have lost contact following a missile launched from Iran.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts launch into space on a mission to the moon and back
video:video.video:description: Four Artemis II astronauts launched into space from Florida's Kennedy Space Center for humanity’s first trip to the moon in more than half a century.

video:video.video:title: Cuba releasing 2,010 prisoners as the US pressures the island's government
video:video.video:description: Families wrapped their arms around freed loved ones outside Cuban prisons on Friday. The outpouring of joy from families comes the day after Cuba’s government said it was going to release 2,010 prisoners in what it said was “humanitarian gestures” ahead of Holy Week.

video:video.video:title: Iranian Red Crescent video shows aftermath of what it says is US-Israeli strike on its warehouse
video:video.video:description: The Iranian Red Crescent on Friday posted a video on their social media account of a destroyed aid warehouse belonging to the organization in the Bushehr province, after it was hit by an American-Israeli airstrike.

video:video.video:title: Artemis astronauts woke up the voice of the late Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell
video:video.video:description: On day 6 of their mission to the moon, the Artemis II astronauts woke up to the voice of Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell, who recorded the message just two months before his death last August.

video:video.video:title: Israeli jets bombard Beirut’s southern suburbs with multiple strikes
video:video.video:description: Israeli jets bombard Beirut’s southern suburbs with multiple strikes

video:video.video:title: Are we alone? Why the question of alien life keeps returning
video:video.video:description: Are we alone? From Trump’s push to release UFO files to new NASA missions, interest in alien life is back in the spotlight. Here’s why.

video:video.video:title: Savannah Guthrie returns to 'Today' anchor desk for first time since mother's disappearance
video:video.video:description: Savannah Guthrie has returned to NBC’s “Today” show anchor desk for the first time since her mother's disappearance more than two months ago.

video:video.video:title: Florida spectators watch as four astronauts embark on historic moon flight
video:video.video:description: Four astronauts have embarked on a high-stakes flight around the moon. Spectators at Space View Park in Titusville cheered as the 32-story moon rocket blasted off from Florida’s Kennedy Space Center.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, April 4th: Afghan quake kills at least 12 people; Russia and Ukraine trade strikes as Zelenskyy travels to Turkey; Fire at a gas lighter factory in Bangladesh kills 5 people; Iraq's soccer team celebrates after World Cup qualification.

video:video.video:title: South Africa hit by record diesel price hikes despite fuel levy cut, as impact felt of Iran war
video:video.video:description: South African motorists on Tuesday headed to gas stations in large numbers in anticipation of record fuel hikes that will come into effect at midnight, as the world continues to feel impacts of the Iran war that has affected global oil and fuel prices.

video:video.video:title: Augmented reality gives trolls a tech upgrade in Norway
video:video.video:description: A troll museum in Norway has combined national folklore with modern AR technology to show visitors a new dimension of the fairy-tale creatures.

video:video.video:title: Vanderbilt's Shea Ralph is the AP Coach of the Year
video:video.video:description: Shea Ralph made Vanderbilt one of the top teams in the nation this season and was honored as The Associated Press women's basketball Coach of the Year on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Critically endangered bongos to be sent to Kenya from European zoos
video:video.video:description: A group of critically endangered mountain bongos bred in European zoos are being prepared for transfer back to Kenya, as part of a long term effort to rebuild wild populations of one of Africa’s rarest large mammals.

video:video.video:title: Oregon Zoo welcomes two penguin chicks
video:video.video:description: The Oregon Zoo welcomed two Humboldt penguin chicks that hatched about a week apart in March. Both are healthy and will nest with their parents until they are 3 months old.

video:video.video:title: 'I'm the space plumber' - astronaut Christina Koch fixes the toilet aboard Artemis II
video:video.video:description: One of the astronauts, Christina Koch, said there was an issue with the toilet but she acted as the plumber when the time came.

video:video.video:title: US retail sales rose more than expected in February
video:video.video:description: Shoppers increased their spending in February after pulling back at the start of the year, reflecting volatile and cautious activity by American consumers even before the Iran war sent gasoline prices soaring.

video:video.video:title: Apollo astronauts brought these items to the moon's surface
video:video.video:description: As Artemis II astronauts head to the moon for a fly-by, here are some Apollo items that have touched the lunar surface. (AP video/Shelby Lum)

video:video.video:title: State TV reports on Iran president asking Americans in letter if war is in their interest
video:video.video:description: President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday issued a letter to the American people, asking them whether their government was really acting in their best interests.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s White House ballroom gets final approval days after a judge ordered a halt to construction
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's new White House ballroom has gotten final approval from a key commission. This comes after a federal judge ordered a halt to construction unless Congress approves the project.

video:video.video:title: Turkey is striving to bring about an end to the 'unlawful, meaningless' war in Iran, Erdogan says
video:video.video:description: Turkey’s President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said his country has intensified a push to end "the unlawful, meaningless" war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Meet the crew behind NASA's latest mission to the moon
video:video.video:description: NASA is preparing for its first crewed mission towards the moon in more than fifty years. The Artemis II mission will not land on the lunar surface, but it's a critical step in testing deep space travel and the crew reflects a new generation of international and more diverse human spaceflight.

video:video.video:title: A priceless ancient golden helmet stolen from a Dutch museum is recovered
video:video.video:description: A priceless ancient golden helmet from Romania stolen last year from a museum in the Netherlands has been recovered, Dutch authorities announced Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Key commission set to vote on whether to approve Trump's new White House ballroom
video:video.video:description: A key commission is expected to vote on Thursday on whether to approve President Donald Trump's new White House ballroom.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter watches four-time champion Italy miss out on World Cup
video:video.video:description: Four-time champion Italy failed to qualify for a third straight World Cup after getting beat in a penalty shootout with 10 men at 66th-ranked Bosnia and Herzegovina in the European playoffs on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Artemis crew can use the toilet as usual, NASA says, after reporting smell from bathroom
video:video.video:description: The toilet on Artemis II can be used as normal, entry flight director Rick Henfling said. It malfunctioned following Wednesday's liftoff and has been hit-or-miss ever since.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts rocket toward the moon after spending a day around Earth
video:video.video:description: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts have fired their engines and are blazing toward the moon. The so-called translunar ignition came 25 hours after liftoff, putting the three Americans and one Canadian on course for a lunar fly-around early next week.

video:video.video:title: Exclusive: Orbán challenger Magyar says election is a 'referendum' on Hungary's place in the world
video:video.video:description: Hungarian opposition leader Péter Magyar said Thursday that a crucial election next week will be a "referendum” on the country’s future.

video:video.video:title: Marilyn Monroe’s final signed check offers insight into her final day
video:video.video:description: A signed check by Marilyn Monroe is among items displayed ahead of an upcoming exhibit. Collector Bryan Johns of the Icon Collection says the document suggests Monroe was excited for the future on the day of her death.

video:video.video:title: Don Cheadle, Ayo Edebiri set for inclusive Broadway debut in ‘Proof’
video:video.video:description: Don Cheadle and Ayo Edebiri reflect on the significance of their Broadway debuts in a revival of “Proof,” David Auburn’s Tony Award and Pulitzer Prize-winning play.

video:video.video:title: United Nations dispatching special envoy to Middle East
video:video.video:description: United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres says the spiral of death and destruction in the Middle East must stop.

video:video.video:title: Spring lambs audition for starring role in London's sheep drive
video:video.video:description: Spring lambs at a farm in the UK are "auditioning" for a starring role in London. The newborn lambs could soon be crossing one of London's busy bridges across the river Thames as part of the annual London Sheep Drive, a tradition dating back to medieval times.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters tackles flames after Russia strikes port infrastructure in Odesa
video:video.video:description: Russia has struck the Black Sea port city of Odesa with drones overnight, hitting civilian and port infrastructure, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Earthquake off Indonesia topples buildings
video:video.video:description: The undersea magnitude 7.4 earthquake toppled buildings in parts of northern Indonesia, sent people fleeing from their homes, killed at least one person and generated a small tsunami Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti hopes 'The Testaments' will inspire Gen-Z to feel empowered and 'enact change'
video:video.video:description: Chase Infiniti stars in the highly anticipated "Handmaid's Tale" spin-off "The Testaments" and hopes the new series will inspire Gen Z to create change in their own communities.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese military withdraws from border towns as Israeli military pushes inwards
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese military said its forces have largely withdrawn from some remaining border towns as Israeli troops continue to push through southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday April 6th: U.S. and Iran received draft proposal for ceasefire; More strikes in Iran and Israel; Wounded U.S. aviator rescued; Artemis II to swing around Moon on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather for festival celebrating Japanese culture in Ohio
video:video.video:description: An organization dedicated to bringing together Ohio’s Japanese and American communities hosted a festival Sunday with a stage performances and other activities related to Japanese culture.

video:video.video:title: Cherry blossom trees falling triggers safety concern as Japan marks the season
video:video.video:description: Many of Tokyo's popular and iconic Somei Yoshino cherry blossom trees were planted during Japan's postwar advancement in the 1960s, and are now getting old and frail.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV remembers his predecessor in Easter Monday blessing
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV remembered his predecessor Pope Francis’s "profound witness of faith and love" during the traditional Easter Monday blessing.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Pooh Shiesty charged with kidnapping over alleged dispute involving rapper Gucci Mane's label
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors in Texas accuse rapper Pooh Shiesty and eight others of robbing three men at gunpoint and kidnapping them following a contract dispute in January involving rapper Gucci Mane’s record label

video:video.video:title: South Africans react to ‘Lion King’ composer suing comedian
video:video.video:description: A Grammy-award winning South African composer who wrote and performed the iconic opening chant in “Circle of Life” for Disney's “The Lion King" movies is suing a comedian for allegedly damaging his reputation by intentionally misrepresenting the song's meaning.

video:video.video:title: Iran to take 'serious legal steps' to protect its cultural sites
video:video.video:description: Iranian Minister of Cultural Heritage, Tourism and Handicrafts Sayed Reza Salihi-Amiri said on Sunday that Iran would take "serious legal steps" to protect its cultural sites, after Israeli and U.S. bombardments have damaged over 100 sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: Trump says it would take 4 hours to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump described the consequences that Iran would face if it didn’t reach a deal with the U.S. by Trump’s 8 p.m. Eastern Tuesday deadline.

video:video.video:title: Blind Massachusetts man is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets
video:video.video:description: Matthew Shifrin is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets using free accessibility instructions created by sighted and blind volunteers in his nonprofit group.

video:video.video:title: Pressure mounts on Kanye West to be pulled from headline role at summer music festival in London
video:video.video:description: Pressure was mounting Sunday on the American rapper Kanye West to be pulled from his headline role at a London music festival this summer.

video:video.video:title: Jon Hamm compares working on 'The Morning Show' with 'Your Friends and Neighbors'
video:video.video:description: Jon Hamm compares the “murderer’s row” of talent on "The Morning Show" to the Broadway-caliber lineup on "Your Friends and Neighbors." (April 15)
